item.title: A woman seated near the floor of Frost Bank Center …
item.description: A woman seated near the floor of Frost Bank Center brought a fake MVP trophy mocking the Thunder star’s reputation of embellishing contact, turning one of the NBA postseason’s loudest debates into a courtside prop during the Western Conference finals.

item.title: Spurs hold the Thunder to 82 points, fewest they've …
item.description: Dan Weiss: Spurs hold the Thunder to 82 points, fewest they've scored in the regular season or playoff since Dec. 2, 2021.

item.title: Robert Williams picking up buzz as a free agent
item.description: Now, Wemby's a freak, I get it. He's a once-in-a-lifetime sort of talent. But, is there anybody out there that the Lakers could sign and go, "At least now you've got a competitive big man out there." Bobby Marks: "The one name that picked up the most buzz, a lot of teams in Chicago last week, was Robert Williams. That's the name. You have to be cautious with that because of his games availability, the minutes. That's the one guy, I don't want to say neutralized Victor Wembanyama, but I think gua

item.title: "I don't know if I'm hard on myself. We all have high …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Wemby: "I don't know if I'm hard on myself. We all have high standards and I know I have a lot of responsibilities but I'm here for it. I'm an employee to play basketball, but I'm not an employee to talk to whoever and find out answers"

item.title: "Doesn't seem like you make a whole lot of halfcourt …
item.description: Reporter: "Doesn't seem like you make a whole lot of halfcourt shots when you're messing around after practices." Wemby: "I wasn't messing around at halftime."

item.title: "What is one emotion that you utilize the most?" …
item.description: ‪NBA‬: Q: "What is one emotion that you utilize the most?" WEMBY: "Passion." Victor Wembanyama (33 PTS) and the Spurs win Game 4 to even the series at 2-2 in the West Finals!

item.title: Wemby's buzzer beater came from 40-feet, the …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Wemby's buzzer beater came from 40-feet, the 2nd-longest made shot of any player this postseason.

item.title: "He's been ridiculous, all playoffs, with the things …
item.description: ‪Tom Orsborn‬: In a gritty, tough-minded performance, Fox shook off his sprained right ankle to deliver a 12-point, 10-rebound double-double: "He's been ridiculous, all playoffs, with the things he's had to play through and what he's given us," Mitch Johnson said.

item.title: De'Aaron Fox on Gregg Popovich after Game 3 loss: That was the first time he walked in and said that's BS
item.description: De'Aaron Fox: "We lost Game 3... but that was the first time he [Gregg Popovich] walked into the locker room and was like "Nah, that's BS.. that's not how we play basketball" and obviously he had some choice words for us..."

item.title: Devin Vassell: 'Experience does not matter'
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Devin Vassell: "Experience does not matter. We're here, we've had all the experience we've needed this regular season, and we're going to keep proving everybody wrong. Whatever they got to say, it really don't matter. We're here now, and we'll see you in OKC"

item.title: "He may be our most consistent player if you took away …
item.description: ‪Tom Orsborn‬: Mitch Johnson said Vassell (13 points, 6 rebounds, 3 assists, 1 steal, 1 block) was "phenomenal." "He may be our most consistent player if you took away any stats and just watched us play," the coach said.

item.description: Chet Holmgren addressed Oklahoma City’s struggles after the San Antonio Spurs delivered a 103-82 win in Game 4 of the Western Conference finals on Sunday night at Frost Bank Center, tying the series at 2-2. “They just played like their season was on the line and we didn’t,” Holmgren said, pointing directly to the Spurs’ level of urgency in a game where Victor Wembanyama finished with 33 points, eight rebounds, five assists, and three blocks. Holmgren said Oklahoma City failed to meet the require

item.title: Overall, Oklahoma City and San Antonio are the only …
item.description: Overall, Oklahoma City and San Antonio are the only other playoff teams with a double-digit positive net rating, and that’s even after they’ve spent the last four games smashing each other’s heads in. The Thunder had a plus-16.9 net rating against the Lakers and plus-18.1 against the Suns; the Spurs were plus-12.9 against Portland and plus-15.9 against Minnesota, even with Victor Wembanyama missing time in both series. Because of that, it’s fair to say that the Knicks should be an underdog in th

item.title: Eddie Johnson: I would have scored 25,000 points based …
item.description: Hoop Central: Fox was called for a foul on Shai Gilgeous-Alexander here. Eddie Johnson: I would have scored 25,000 points based on this occurrence. I got punched in stomach, squeezed on my hip and pushed, tapped on elbow. Etc….

item.title: Jalen Williams making 'some progress' in on-court workouts
item.description: NBA on ESPN: .@ShamsCharania provides an injury update on the Thunder's Jalen Williams and Ajay Mitchell: The Thunder are still evaluating both Jay Williams and AJ Mitchell on a day to day basis. J-Dub, aka Jalen Williams, we know he suffered a second injury to that same left hamstring in just under one month. I am told that he is making some progress in his on-court workouts. He's starting to do more and more. AJ Mitchell, on the other hand, he's been dealing with right calf tightness throughou

item.title: A group of nuns seated near the floor at Frost Bank …
item.description: Before Game 4 of the Western Conference finals tipped off Sunday night in San Antonio, the Spurs received an early blessing. A group of nuns seated near the floor at Frost Bank Center was shown on the arena video board blessing Spurs center Luke Kornet before the matchup against the Oklahoma City Thunder, drawing one of the loudest crowd reactions of the night before the game even started.

item.title: Kornet is a devout Catholic who used to regularly …
item.description: The group of nuns is part of a community of Spurs fans from the San Antonio chapter of the Salesian Sisters of St. John Bosco. They used to appear regularly at Spurs events over the past couple of decades, though they had not attended a game this season. Some fans credit them for the Spurs’ draft luck, as the nuns root for and pray for the Spurs. Kornet is a devout Catholic who used to regularly write about his faith on his blog. In an interview with the website Sojourners, one of the nuns, Sist

item.title: Ajay Mitchell out for Game 5
item.description: Tim MacMahon: OKC’s Jalen Williams is listed as questionable again for Game 5, but the injury designation has been changed from left hamstring soreness to strain. Ajay Mitchell (soleus) is out again.

item.title: Fox and Harper have done a good job fighting through …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: #Spurs have a clean injury report ahead of Game 5 on Tuesday night in Oklahoma City. Fox and Harper have done a good job fighting through their ailments, or as Mitch Johnson put it recently: "They're competing their ass off and they're not 100%."

item.title: In the video teaser above, from a French TV show, Tony …
item.description: Maxime Aubin: In the video teaser above, from a French TV show, Tony Parker seems to downplay Greg Popovich’s impact before he joined the Spurs in 2001, taking a shot at the 1999 championship in particular: “I mean, Pop was just getting started (in 1999), it was only his fourth year. (…) He hadn’t been named NBA Coach of the Year (…) (A ring?) I mean a ring that everybody criticized. Come on, let’s put things in perspective here.”

item.title: "I voted for Wembanyama third for MVP, and I actually …
item.description: On X (formerly Twitter), Termine detailed his decision not to vote Wembanyama for the First Team list amid backlash from fans. He explained that he opted for the traditional way of voting rather than complying with the recent rule change that allows positionless voting. He believes that it would be unfair to adapt to the new rule, considering what several other legendary centers of the past have endured in the All-NBA voting. "I voted for Wembanyama third for MVP, and I actually think he's a bet

item.title: "They changed the rule recently because [Joel] Embiid …
item.description: "But this year, he was the second-best center, and I vote by position. In my opinion, it is unfair historically to those who came before us, whom I have great respect for, that centers were not allowed to be on the First Team at the same time. It’s unfair to Wilt [Chamberlain], [Bill] Russell, and others who couldn’t make First Team together just because they played the same position. "They changed the rule recently because [Joel] Embiid had been the second-best player for a couple of years but

item.title: The 2025-26 Knicks will have the best point …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Barring a beatdown in the Finals, the 2025-26 Knicks will have the best point differential in NBA playoffs history. The top 3 all-time as of now: 2026 Knicks: +271 2017 Warriors: +230 2014 Spurs: +214 Whoa.

item.title: Danny Green: "A huge presence. It doesn't go away. …
item.description: Danny, you won a championship under Pop. You heard De'Aaron Fox. What are those Pop speeches like? How big of a presence does he still have on this franchise and this team?" Danny Green: "A huge presence. It doesn't go away. He's just one of those guys that you're fearful of, regardless of age or health. I'm still scared of him to this day. So when he walks in a room, it's business and it's serious. So he came in, I'm sure he left an imprint on them. He let them know who's messing up, who needs

item.title: “The way we’re playing now, I don’t think it matters …
item.description: These Knicks remind Clyde Frazier and Spike Lee of the versions in the 1970s. So to no surprise, both are predicting an end to the 53-year championship drought — regardless of whether the opponent is the Thunder or Spurs. “The way we’re playing now, I don’t think it matters who we play,” Frazier said after the Knicks beat the Cavs 130-93 on Monday, advancing to their first NBA Finals since 1999. “Their suffocating defense, the ball movement. [Coach Mike] Brown came in wanting to run pace and spa

item.title: Steve Kerr explored the possibility of coaching in Paris before re-signing with the Warriors
item.description: A nine-time NBA champion in the French capital? For a few weeks, Paris believed it. As confirmed to L'Équipe , Steve Kerr, the coach with five championship rings as a player (from 1996 to 1998 with Michael Jordan's Chicago Bulls, in 1999 and in 2003 with the San Antonio Spurs), then four as coach of Golden State (2015, 2017, 2018 and 2022), considered the prospect. Even though the hypothesis never turned into formal negotiation, with the Beirut native ultimately signing a two-year extension wit

item.title: A Victor Wembanyama rookie card that was previously …
item.description: A Victor Wembanyama rookie card that was previously the subject of some controversy sold in a private deal for $5.11 million, making it the most expensive known sale of a non-autographed NBA card. The sale of the San Antonio Spurs center’s 2023-24 Panini Prizm one-of-one Black parallel rookie card, which has a Gem-Mint PSA 10 grade, was brokered by Fanatics Collect. It’s the fourth highest publicly known sale of an NBA card, according to online card sales database Card Ladder. The buyer, who spo

item.title: “The notion that everyone is just gonna accept it is …
item.description: Yes, the conversation among rival GMs and coaches right now is much like the public conversation—filled with awe and respect for Wemby and Shai, and for what the Spurs and Thunder have built. But it’s tinged with defiance. No one is conceding the next half-decade. “The notion that everyone is just gonna accept it is insane,” said an executive from an Eastern Conference playoff team. “Everybody that are in these jobs are competitive. They’re not just gonna accept it. A team like San Antonio, who

item.title: “When I watch the game, I’m not watching it with any …
item.description: Both the message and the tone were consistent among a half-dozen executives and coaches who spoke to The Ringer. No one can say exactly how they’ll dethrone the two Western Conference powers—just that they’re committed to finding a way. “When I watch the game, I’m not watching it with any fear; I’m not watching it with a level of like, ‘We’re in a lot of trouble,’” said a coach with a Western Conference playoff team. “I’m watching it going: This is the standard. We’re going to have to find ways

item.title: “People are gonna figure it out, how to beat them,” …
item.description: “People are gonna figure it out, how to beat them,” said the first Eastern Conference executive. “With the rules the way they are, San Antonio may not be able to keep all those guys. So a window opens up again. You have to be prepared for when that window is there.”

item.title: Louis Williams: "We keep talking about (Gregg …
item.description: Louis Williams: "We keep talking about (Gregg Popovich). At some point, when do we give credit to Coach (Mitch) Johnson? Like, when do we give him his proper due for being a part of this? Like I love the fact that Pop's involved. I love the fact that he's there. And this's not even a critique. I'm literally asking the question because I just feel like Mitch Johnson should get way more credit than he gets. And I understand the culture, I understand the organization and Pop is like the Godfather.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama trading card sells for $5.11 million
item.description: Victor Wembanyama's 1-of-1 numbered 2023-24 Panini Prizm Black parallel has sold for $5.11 million in a private sale via Fanatics Collect, Fanatics confirmed to ESPN. It's by far the most ever spent on a Wembanyama card, and the fourth and 11th most spent on a basketball and a sports card, respectively. It's also the most expensive known sale of a non-autographed NBA card and the third sports card sold for $5 million in 2026.

item.title: But right now, when you look at the Giannis market, I …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: "I think that Giannis's market is going to be a little softer than, you know, than maybe some people expect, unless Oklahoma City loses to San Antonio and then just decides, all right, we're going all in, maybe that would be a a shift, a shift in the market that would really sort of drive the price up. But right now, when you look at the Giannis market, I think the Bucks are in a tough spot because everyone knows that they have to move Giannis. Like this whole sort of charade of

item.description: Clemente Almanza: Mark Daigneault on their Game 4 shot quality: “It was our worst offensive game by a mile from a process standpoint.”

item.description: Tom Orsborn: Mitch on how he and his staff work to get better: “You play a lot of hypotheticals and throw a lot of things against the wall and argue with your staff just for the sake of arguing and make each other think a little bit more…”

He averaged 25.5 points and 7.8 assists in the series. The Knicks will face defending champion Oklahoma City or San Antonio in the finals. “I speak from experience,” Hammon said Tuesday. “Allen Iverson got MVP and he lost in the finals. I think the two best teams are probably in the West, but I’m up for being

item.title: Thunder take 3-2 series lead with big win over Spurs
item.description: Justin Martinez: Final: Thunder 127, Spurs 114 Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: 32 points and 9 assists Alex Caruso: 22 points and 6 assists Chet Holmgren: 16 points and 11 boards OKC claims a 3-2 series lead on a night when it didn't have Jalen Williams and Ajay Mitchell. It can book its ticket to the NBA Finals on Thursday.

item.title: Mitch Johnson waves the white flag. Thunder are gonna …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Mitch Johnson waves the white flag. Thunder are gonna take Game 5 and a 3-2 series lead. Massive game from the reserves, basically the inverse of last game all around. Chet’s best game of the series. Caruso and McCain were huge. Probably Wemby’s least impactful game of the series

item.title: Wembanyama scored 20 points, his lowest of the series, …
item.description: Now down 3-2 in the series, they'll try to extend the matchup — and save their season — in Game 6 at San Antonio on Thursday night. Wembanyama scored 20 points, his lowest of the series, and only a 12-for-12 effort from the foul line helped him get there in Game 5. He was 4 for 15 from the floor, missing all five of his 3-point tries, never seeming to get into any sort of rhythm. He offered an impassioned speech to teammates during a timeout barely two minutes into the third quarter, after the T

item.title: think they wanted the game more than we did,” he added
item.description: ‪Tom Orsborn‬: Champagnie said OKC was the “more desperate team.” “I think they wanted the game more than we did,” he added.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama declined to speak with media after Game 5
item.description: Sam Amick: Victor Wembanyama, who struggled mightily in the Spurs’ Game 5 loss in OKC, declined to do media this evening. He just left the PayCom Center as midnight central time nears.

item.title: "He's got to take more than 15 shots," Johnson said. …
item.description: A team spokesman said Wembanyama would be unavailable to speak to reporters, nearly an hour after Spurs coach Mitch Johnson said "we're going to need to" get San Antonio's generational superstar going to stave off elimination Thursday in Game 6 with the Thunder now leading the series 3-2. "He's got to take more than 15 shots," Johnson said. "Even with the [12] free throws, he's going to have to score more than 20 points for sure."

item.title: Victor Wemanyama had only 1 assist and 2 turnovers …
item.description: Iztok Franko: We've seen Harden's assists to turnover stats floating around throughout the playoffs. Victor Wemanyama had only 1 assist and 2 turnovers last night. He had as many (or more) turnovers as assists in 9 of 15 games in the playoffs and is averaging 2.8 turnovers and 2.8 assists.

item.title: Devin Vassell summed up the defensive collapse this …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Devin Vassell summed up the defensive collapse this way: “Undisciplined. Undisciplined rotations, undisciplined fouls. That's about it.”

item.title: Fox acknowledges he's a man who thrives off routine, …
item.description: As teammates wearing Nike tech fleece, jeans and hoodies scurry through the locker room to board SUVs and buses awaiting outside that will take the San Antonio Spurs to an optional team dinner after their Game 3 victory over the Minnesota Timberwolves in the conference semifinals, Fox stuffs both hands into his pockets and shakes his head. The 28-year-old "old man" of this young group has made up his mind. "It's already 12:30. I will not be at the dinner," Fox said. "We can do brunch tomorrow."

item.title: "It allows me to be more consistent, more …
item.description: Fox says he firmly believes the steadier his life is off the court, the more his game can grow. It's how he continues to rise every time he falls fighting through a high right ankle sprain that has hobbled him since Game 4 against the Wolves on May 10. "It allows me to be more consistent, more levelheaded," Fox told ESPN. "If I'm not making shots, I feel like I don't get sporadic. Even when I'm making shots, my game doesn't change. I think I stay kind of level, through it all."

item.title: “The way I see it, when you’re in it, you have to look …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Tracy McGrady asked Chet Holmgren if he views Wemby as a rival for years to come: “The way I see it, when you’re in it, you have to look at it as Thunder vs Spurs. We’re trying to win these games. They’re trying to win these games. That’s how you have to attack it”

item.title: Mason Plumlee after trucking Jared McCain in the back, …
item.description: Mason Plumlee after trucking Jared McCain in the back, "I got another one for you too."

item.title: “The refs are cheating,” Ross wrote on social media. …
item.description: Throughout this year’s NBA playoffs, fans have ripped countless referees for apparently giving the Thunder preferential treatment. Well, it appears a former first-round pick is now entertaining that conversation. Terrence Ross, who played in the NBA from 2012-2023, accused the refs of “cheating” during the Western Conference Finals. “The refs are cheating,” Ross wrote on social media. “Point blank period.”

item.title: The OnlyFans model, who goes by BlueBearI, took to X, …
item.description: ‪After one of the women who is frequently seen behind San Antonio Spurs coach Mitch Johnson was identified as an OnlyFans (OF) model, their status has been revealed for Game 6 against the Oklahoma City Thunder. The OnlyFans model, who goes by BlueBearI, took to X, formerly Twitter, before the Spurs' Game 5 loss the OKC, to reveal her status along with the other woman, Julie J Swan, for the next contest. “Yes, we will be at Game 6,” Blue wrote.

item.title: NBA issues warning to Victor Wembanyama after skipping media obligations
item.description: Jeff McDonald: The NBA has officially warned Victor Wembanyama about violating media access rules, per a league source. Wembanyama declined to address the media after the Spurs' Game 5 loss last night in OKC.

item.title: The NBA’s rationale for not fining Victor Wembanyama, …
item.description: Sam Amick: The NBA’s rationale for not fining Victor Wembanyama, and instead issuing him a warning, took into account that he’s considered a first-time offender on this front. That part is standard procedure, though I did think they might come down harder on him because of the magnitude of the moment (it’s…the West Finals).

item.title: Turquoise, pink and orange words mount an exhibit of …
item.description: Turquoise, pink and orange words mount an exhibit of books immediately noticeable when patrons walk into San Antonio’s Central Library, the bright red building in the middle of the city’s downtown: “Read Like Wemby.” Just to the right of the circulation desk, where people can sign up for a library card and check out books, are some of San Antonio Spurs star Victor Wembanyama’s favorite books. Many nod to his love for science fiction and fantasy literature. Some of the books on display include “Y

item.title: When veteran forward Harrison Barnes, another avid …
item.description: When veteran forward Harrison Barnes, another avid reader, first came to the Spurs, he and Wembanyama started a book club that included a couple of other players until it eventually whittled down to just the two of them. Barnes made sure to include books from Wembanyama’s favorite genre, science fiction, and more specifically a favorite author, Sanderson. Wembanyama has visited Alien Worlds, a comic book store in San Antonio, “upon occasion,” said David Wheeler, the CEO of Dragon’s Lair, a comic

item.title: Though Wembanyama has not participated in the …
item.description: Though Wembanyama has not participated in the campaign, the SAPL hopes he might eventually. The goal is to inspire people of all ages, especially children, to read more. It shows that someone can be one of the best basketball players in the world and also love reading. They want the world to know that reading is cool. Wemby is cool. Science fiction and fantasy and world-building and magic are cool.

item.title: Transit riders traveling to Madison Square Garden for …
item.description: NJ Transit riders traveling to Madison Square Garden for the Knicks game at 8:30 p.m. June 16, against the Thunder or Spurs, will need to switch to PATH trains to get into Manhattan at the 33rd Street station, a spokesperson for NJ Transit said. Asked if the policy could be tweaked due to the potential World Cup-NBA Finals crossover, a spokesperson said no changes will be allowed.

item.title: Mason Plumlee's foul on Jared McCain upgraded to a Flagrant 1
item.description: NBA Official: Mason Plumlee’s (SAS) foul against Jared McCain (OKC) at 1:39 of the 4th quarter in Game 5 of the Western Conference Finals on 5/26/26 has been upgraded to a Flagrant 1 after league review.

item.title: Thunder injury report remains the same:
item.description: Tim MacMahon: Thunder injury report remains the same: Ajay Mitchell (soleus) out, Jalen Williams (hamstring) questionable

item.title: Spurs' injury report remains empty heading into Game 6 …
item.description: Jared Weiss: The Spurs' injury report remains empty heading into Game 6 of the Western Conference Finals, down 3-2 to the Thunder.

item.title: Of SGA's 131 points in this series, 49 (37.4%) have …
item.description: Dan Weiss: Of SGA's 131 points in this series, 49 (37.4%) have come from the foul line, far exceeding his percentage in the regular season (25.5%) & even in the first two rounds of the playoffs (31.7%) vs. the Suns & Lakers. Spurs have had success when they keep SGA off the line.

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander sends cease and desist letter to Underdog
item.description: Underdog Sports, a sports prediction market and fantasy sports company, received a cease and desist letter regarding a promotion that makes fun of Shai Gilgeous-Alexander’s efforts to draw fouls. Underdog created a version of the board game Operation in which the buzzer went off for a foul anytime Gilgeous-Alexander was touched. The game was called Unethical Hoops. The company had a contest to give away 100 copies of the game during Game 3 of the Western Conference finals between Gilgeous-Alexan

item.title: "He was voted by the pro basketball writers …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Chris Haynes on why Wemby wasn't fined for skipping his postgame presser: "He was voted by the pro basketball writers association for being one of the most accommodating players to media. They let him off the hook this time"

item.title: Steve Kerr, Gregg Popovich — they were guys that went …
item.description: “You know, Steve Kerr, Gregg Popovich — they were guys that went deep into their bench, and they both always used to say, ‘It's not about now, it's about the postseason,’” Brown said after Game 3. “You know, you keep guys engaged by doing that, and you do develop not just the bench, but the team as well, because guys get used to playing with other guys, just in case someone goes down.”

item.title: “There is no playbook for midseason changes in the …
item.description: Said Spurs veteran forward Harrison Barnes about Mitch Johnson: “Mitch stepped in and he had it. We’ve had a lot of conversations about a lot of different things. Never once did I feel like the moment might be too big, [or that] he doesn’t know what he is talking about. I’ve had full trust in him. I’d run through a wall for him. “There is no playbook for midseason changes in the case of what it was and the gravity of what it was. To be able to do that, that was one of the most impressive things

item.title: “We do things a little bit different than [last …
item.description: Spurs forward Keldon Johnson also noted he respects how the Spurs coach holds players accountable no matter the score or who they are. For example, Mitch Johnson told the media after Game 5 of the Western Conference finals that Wembanyama needs to shoot more after a lackluster performance. Also, Johnson nearly brought rookie forward Carter Bryant to tears in Game 4 after he strongly reprimanded him for a bad turnover. “We do things a little bit different than [last season],” Keldon Johnson said.

item.title: The reason why he was not penalized for skipping media …
item.description: NBA insider Chris Haynes explained why the league decided not to punish the Spurs superstar despite missing his required media availability. "I was told that the league contacted Wemby and gave him a warning and said that moving forward they will have to get him fined marginally for skipping out on media, but the reason why he was not penalized for skipping media after last night's games is that he was just, he was voted by the Pro Basketball Writers Association for being one of the most accommo

item.title: Victor Wembanyama also needs to be a dominant …
item.description: Victor Wembanyama also needs to be a dominant defensive force, considering Oklahoma City connected on 49% of its shots with him on the court in Game 5, which ranks as the best field goal percentage a team has achieved so far this postseason against the 22-year old, according to ESPN Research. Widely proclaimed the best player currently in the NBA, Wembanyama needs to show it for San Antonio to stave off elimination. And though he has shocked observers with a seemingly otherworldly skill set, Wem

item.title: “I’m honored that — something I’m going to tell my …
item.description: Just as Curry was an evolution of him, Reggie Miller sees Wembanyama as an evolution of the bigs that came before. Miller now hopes that, as part of the NBC revival in his second career as a broadcaster, the players who leave their mark will remember that he was a part of the journey, too. “I’m honored that — something I’m going to tell my kids — I called Victor Wembanayama’s first playoff game,” Miller says. “What’s going to be cool is some of my best moments as a basketball player were on NBC.

item.title: "Victor Wembanyama, one of the rare few capable of …
item.description: Ricky Rubio: "Victor Wembanyama, one of the rare few capable of changing the NBA game, like Stephen Curry, Michael Jordan, or Shaquille O'Neal."

item.title: Victor Wembanyama becomes fifth player to accumulate 350 points, 150 rebounds in first playoff appearance
item.description: Michael C. Wright: Victor Wembanyama now ranks as the fifth player to produce 350 points & 150 rebounds in his first career postseason appearance, per ESPN Research.

item.title: Wembanyama hit three 3-pointers in the first quarter, …
item.description: Michael C. Wright: Wembanyama hit three 3-pointers in the first quarter, his most in any quarter of his postseason career, per ESPN Research.

item.title: Jalen Williams is active for Game 6
item.description: Tim MacMahon: Jalen Williams is active, but OKC’s starting lineup remains the same as Game 5.

item.title: When he can be aggressive, it's very impactful."
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Mitch Johnson said Dylan Harper’s injury, OKC’s defense have combined to slow him down in recent games. “For the most part I give a lot of acknowledgement to what OKC is doing, and Dylan's had some really good moments as well. ... When he can be aggressive, it's very impactful."

item.title: That’s not the plan. We’d like to be clean, but we'd …
item.description: Clemente Almanza: Mark Daigneault on OKC’s offense: “I thought what our approach did in Game 5 was it gave us a margin for error to be a little bit sloppy, and we were. That’s not the plan. We’d like to be clean, but we'd much rather approach the game like that.”

item.title: "They got all the marks of a great defense. They've …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Thunder coach Mark Daigneault on what makes the Spurs defense so tough: "They got all the marks of a great defense. They've got, obviously, the rim protection, speaks for itself, but they have great point of attack defenders that are highly aggressive and that really make you uncomfortable on the perimeter with that behind them. And then they're competent, they've got schemes, they're organized, they're disciplined, they're well coached, so all the all the things you need to be a re

item.title: “We've seen these guys now, these this team 10 times …
item.description: Tom Petrini: “We've seen these guys now, these this team 10 times this year, and we've seen them the last five games that we've played, and we won a couple, and they beat us three times, and so there's no Lombardi speech coming, or magic line. We're going to go out there and try to play our standard of basketball with the proper approach, and try to execute through all the things that we're going to be seeing at that moment.” -Mitch Johnson

item.title: “The takeaway from any of the games that we've won or …
item.description: Clemente Almanza: Mitch Johnson on the officiating talk: “The takeaway from any of the games that we've won or lost has not been that the refs decided it.”

item.title: “Sometimes players make shots or miss shots, …
item.description: Clemente Almanza: Mitch Johnson on how they’ve limited SGA: “Sometimes players make shots or miss shots, especially players of his caliber. You can get at times intoxicated of what you think may be working or not working. I think we've learned that throughout this playoffs.”

item.title: Stephon Castle on Thunder: 'We feel like collectively that we're better than this team'
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Stephon Castle: "We feel like collectively that we're better than this team"

item.title: I don't think scoring against them has been a problem …
item.description: Tom Petrini: "I just think all of our focus and attention was on the defensive end. I don't think scoring against them has been a problem for us. Our self-inflicted mistakes, like turnovers and allowing them to get offense rebounds and easy buckets, is what slows us down. So, I think when we're focused on defense and we're getting stops and being able to get out and run and get easy looks, it makes the game pretty simple for us." -Steph Castle

item.title: "I think a lot of my turnovers were unforced. I know …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Steph Castle on limiting his turnovers: "I think a lot of my turnovers were unforced. I know that they like to pressure a lot, but I don't think a lot of my turnovers came from their pressure. I think it was kind of self-inflicted. Getting in there, taking my time, and playing off two feet is what's been working for me."

item.title: For him to come in and give us a breather and …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Steph Castle on Carter Bryant: "For him to be a rookie and come in and there's no dropoff defensively. That alone says a lot. For him to come in and give us a breather and defensive rebound for us and try and make minimal mistakes is big because we need those minutes. In the playoffs, every possession matters. So, when he's doing that and making it tough on (SGA) and tiring him out, in the long run, it definitely helps."

item.title: Carter has been as coachable as anybody. At times, he …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Asked Mitch Johnson about Carter Bryant's night and his willingness to be coached hard. "There's much to do with what happened a couple games ago. I know, in the visual of it... Carter has been as coachable as anybody. At times, he probably is trying to do the right thing too much, and I think that's where, at times, the urgency and the competitiveness of myself comes out because I just want that kid to play fast and aggressive. He has good raw instincts, and when he plays free and

item.title: "First thing is listening to the experienced people, …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Wemby on his first-ever Game 7: "First thing is listening to the experienced people, whether it's on our team, on our staff, or outside, we got the chance to have plenty of those around."

item.title: "We did what we needed to do, trusted the game, …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Wemby: "We did what we needed to do, trusted the game, trusted the basketball Gods"

item.title: He’s obviously not 100%. He didn’t know what to …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Mark Daigneault on Jalen Williams: "He’s been hellbent on trying to get himself to this point. I give him credit. He’s a big team guy, big competitor. He’s obviously not 100%. He didn’t know what to expect, I didn’t know what to expect. So it was a matter of getting him out there in an insulated role and see what he could bring to the team. … He hasn’t done a full return to play the way that he would.”

item.title: Spurs dominate Thunder, force Game 7
item.description: Clemente Almanza: FINAL: Spurs 118, Thunder 91 SGA - 15 points, 4 assists Holmgren - 10 points, 11 rebounds Hartenstein - 10 points, 5 rebounds Wembanyama - 28 points, 10 rebounds Castle - 17 points, 9 assists Harper - 18 points, 6 rebounds Series is tied 3-3

item.title: OKC never led tonight, making this their first …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: OKC never led tonight, making this their first wire-to-wire loss in the playoffs since 2016 in Game 1 of Thunder/Spurs!

item.title: Spurs win by 27 points, the largest margin of victory …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Spurs win by 27 points, the largest margin of victory in an elimination game in Spurs playoff history.

item.title: This is Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's worst plus-minus …
item.description: Law Murray: This is Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's worst plus-minus (-28) since Game 3 of the 2025 Western Conference Finals, a road game that the Thunder lost by 42 points.

item.title: SGA has scored 20+ points in 140 consecutive regular …
item.description: Dan Weiss: SGA has scored 20+ points in 140 consecutive regular season games, an NBA record. Tonight marks the second time in three games the Spurs have held the two-time MVP below 20.

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: Spurs were the aggressors tonight from start to finish
item.description: Nick Gallo: Shai postgame: "They were the aggressors tonight from start to finish. They played harder than us, hit more shots, were more aggressive, were in attack mode. We were on our heels. We kept it in a manageable spot, but the second half he just blew it out of proportion."

item.title: There was the long robe that the San Antonio star wore …
item.description: There was the long robe that the San Antonio star wore to his home arena on Thursday night, done to celebrate an Islamic holiday but also reminding some of his look last summer at a Shaolin temple he visited while seeking physical, mental and spiritual growth. There was the freshly cropped hair, another sign that he was all business. “I’d seen a picture pregame,” Spurs guard Devin Vassell told NBA TV afterward. “I knew he was locked in from there, for sure.”

item.title: The robe that he wore to Thursday’s game, he confirmed …
item.description: The robe that he wore to Thursday’s game, he confirmed in French during his postgame news conference, wasn’t an homage to his time last June in China at the Shaolin temple — but rather to celebrate Eid al-Adha, an Islamic holiday. And during that same French portion of his remarks, he was asked if he can take a moment to even contemplate how far the Spurs have come. Short answer: no. “I have absolutely no desire to do that right now,” he said.

item.title: Wembanyama is not Muslim,
item.description: Maxime Ausin: I’m seeing a lot of misinformation about Victor Wembanyama’s pregame outfit, even from major American media. @hugo_levay asked the player directly, and Wemby confirmed it was an outfit for Eid al-Adha, the Muslim celebration happening this week, not a monk’s robe. PS: Wembanyama is not Muslim, at least, the last time I asked him, he said he didn’t identify with any particular religion.

item.title: "My little sister called me before the game and told …
item.description: ESPN: "My little sister called me before the game and told me 'You gotta make it to Saturday.' I'm like 'alright I got you.'" Dylan Harper delivered on his promise??

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander vs Spurs this season For a …
item.description: Data4Basket: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander vs Spurs this season For a player averaging 35.8 Produced Points per 40' and 66.5 TS%, San Antonio caused some rare struggles. ?? SGA's worst scoring + efficiency performances of the season came against the Spurs: • 21 Produced Points/40' — 39.0 TS% • 23 Produced Points/40' — 44.7 TS% • 24 Produced Points/40' — 49.0 TS% • 25 Produced Points/40' — 52.4 TS% ?? All ended in OKC losses. The Spurs were the team that most consistently pushed SGA out of his usual el

item.title: "When he's switched on mentally and aggressive, he's …
item.description: ?Tom Orsborn?: Spurs worked hard to get Harper in right mindset after Game 5: "When he's switched on mentally and aggressive, he's pretty damn good," Mitch said. "He's got all the talent in the world, makeup to do whatever he wants. He's capable of those nights when he's in the right place between the ears."

item.title: Peter Holt is in attendance for Game 6. I cannot …
item.description: Raul Dominguez Jr.: Former Spurs owner Peter Holt is in attendance for Game 6. I cannot recall the last time I saw him at a Spurs game.

item.title: Saturday will mark the second Game 7 in NBA history …
item.description: ‪NBA Stat‬: Saturday will mark the second Game 7 in NBA history between two teams who each had a .750+ win pct in the regular season. The other came in the 1981 Eastern Conference Finals, when the Celtics beat the 76ers 91-90 to complete their comeback from a 3-1 series deficit. (via OptaSports) #NBA

item.title: First player in NBA history with 25+ threes 55+ blocks …
item.description: StatMuse: First player in NBA history with 25+ threes 55+ blocks in a postseason.

item.title: Devin Vassell averaged 13.9 points per game this …
item.description: Devin Vassell averaged 13.9 points per game this season with a usage rate (17.6%) that ranked sixth on the team. His teammates talk about him as if he rejected a part in the Marvel Cinematic Universe to play a supporting character on a prestige TV show. What he is doing now "has more substantive value to it," Spurs wing Julian Champagnie said, than averaging 20-plus points on a cellar dweller.

item.title: "I know my game," Vassell said. "And I know I can get …
item.description: On a team stacked with ballhandlers and short on spacing, though, he does most of his damage off the ball. "I know my game," Vassell said. "And I know I can get to my spot whenever I want to, I know I can get a shot whenever I want to. But for the betterment of the team, sometimes that's not what you need."

item.title: "I can't sit there and just be passive when I get the …
item.description: He knew coming in that he'd be in this kind of role. The challenge was figuring out how to be most effective and how to remain aggressive in it. He has to cut and come off screens hard. He has to make quick decisions. He has to be a threat at all times. "I can't sit there and just be passive when I get the ball or, if I have a shot, turn it down because maybe I didn't have the ball for the past couple of minutes," Vassell said. "I still gotta be ready whenever the ball is thrown my way. It might

item.title: Magic seriously interested in Sean Sweeney as new head coach
item.description: League sources have conveyed with increasing volume this week that Orlando's interest in San Antonio's Sean Sweeney to fill its coaching vacancy is more serious than previously known. "They're very interested," said one league coaching source. The Magic's plan, league sources say, was to travel to San Antonio during the Spurs' Western Conference finals showdown with the Thunder to meet face-to-face with Sweeney while he is engaged in an active playoff run.

item.title: Magic to hire Sean Sweeney as head coach
item.description: Michael Scotto: JUST IN: The Orlando Magic will hire Sean Sweeney as their head coach, league sources told @hoopshype. Sweeney was an associate head coach for the San Antonio Spurs. Prior, he was an assistant coach for the Dallas Mavericks, Detroit Pistons, Milwaukee Bucks, and Brooklyn Nets.

item.title: Sweeney will finish the remainder of the postseason on …
item.description: Shams Charania: Sweeney will finish the remainder of the postseason on the Spurs bench -- with Game 7 on Saturday night for an NBA Finals berth. Former Chicago Bulls head coach Billy Donovan and LA Clippers assistant coach Jeff Van Gundy were the two other finalists for the Magic's coaching vacancy, sources said.

item.title: With Victor, he's barely done this sort of thing over …
item.description: Sam Amick on Victor Wembanyama skipping media duties: "It was a bad look because as a player accountability matters. When Tim Duncan would never talk to the media, he would always talk to the media when they lost, because he didn't want his teammates having to sit there and answer questions that he thought that he should be answering. With Victor, he's barely done this sort of thing over his first three years. It was definitely a break from his norm. The people I talked to basically said like he

item.title: I had one of the executives talk about the Jazz, who I …
item.description: Sam Amick: "Now the Wembanyama effect to your question is I actually think it has a domino effect on the Giannis sweepstakes this summer, where there's only so many guys in the league that can give you a puncher's chance at slowing Wembanyama down. You look at the Thunder, they got a guy in Chet Holmgren who at first glance looks like tailor-made to give him some pushback, and he's obviously had a really hard time in this series. So the impact is even beyond getting a guy like that, it's loading

item.title: Jalen Williams will not play in Game 7
item.description: Shams Charania: Oklahoma City Thunder's Jalen Williams has been ruled out for Game 7 of the Western Conference Finals against the San Antonio Spurs.

item.title: Waters said Wembanyama talked about how they were …
item.description: “I don’t remember,” Wembanyama said in his postgame news conference before ignoring a follow-up. He wasn’t interested in the speech going to anyone else but his teammates. Thankfully, teammate Lindy Waters III remembered. “We just got to leave it all out there,” Waters told The Athletic when asked what Wembanyama’s message was. “Back’s against the wall, and we’ve had multiple chances this series to capitalize and we just let it go.” Waters said Wembanyama talked about how they were playing with

item.title: In the first half, they broke out ice coverages to …
item.description: In the first half, they broke out ice coverages to keep SGA on the right side of the floor, so that he couldn’t easily hop into a stepback and would have to completely flip his hips around to get a shot off. It also kept Kornet far enough back that the MVP couldn’t just drive all the way around the coverage to get to the rim or make the kinds of cross-court kick-out passes he doesn’t usually do.

item.title: “You don’t see a lot of guys in their third year, at …
item.description: Yet as veteran forward Harrison Barnes shared, Wembanyama is making all sorts of valuable noise behind the scenes while developing as a leader in ways that are typically not seen. “I think (it’s being) able to speak in these situations, to be able to talk with confidence, to be able to not only lead by example, but to lead with your voice in those moments, right?” Barnes said. “You don’t see a lot of guys in their third year, at this stage, with this level of responsibility that he has on our te

item.title: Tony Parker: Of course. And I even remember the …
item.description: Is that something you sometimes see with big NBA competitors? When that 21st pick goes by, and Joseph Forte gets picked, do you still remember the names of the guys selected before you? Tony Parker: Of course. All of them. Alex: All of them? Tony Parker: Of course. And I even remember the European point guard who was drafted ahead of me at 24 by the Utah Jazz: Raul Lopez. Alex: Raul Lopez. Ah, you remember him. Tony Parker: Yeah. Well, he did not really have an NBA career. Alex: He had a career

item.title: Tony Parker reveals why Gregg Popovich chose Boris Diaw over Tim Duncan in the final second of Finals Game 7
item.description: Bibi: Exactly. LeBron misses his shot, the Spurs play the possession, and I have someone next to me who was not only on the floor, but also on the bench and may have some insight. One of the best rebounders in NBA history is not on the floor. The Spurs are playing smaller. I understand it was to defend the three-point line, but— Tony Parker: It was to defend the switch. It was not to defend the three-point line. It was to defend the switch. Pop preferred having Boris for the switch rather than T

item.title: James Talarico will suspend campaign operations …
item.description: Michael Karlis: TEXAS POLITICS: James Talarico will suspend campaign operations Saturday for game 7 of the Western Conference Finals. “Are y’all ready to beat OKC!?” Talarico asked the 1,000+ crowd at San Antonio’s Paper Tiger.

item.title: There are a lot more teams that have motivation to go …
item.description: Ramona Shelburne: "I think as much grief as the Milwaukee Bucks took for not trading Giannis Antetokounmpo the last year and a half, this postseason has completely reframed the trade discussions for Giannis because of how good Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs are. Every single person watching these playoffs says, maybe we better get Giannis. Maybe we need that guy. I don't know how we're going to compete with the Spurs for the next five to 10 years if we don't have another star like Giannis. Ther

item.title: Referees for tonight’s Spurs-OKC Game 7: Marc Davis, …
item.description: Legion Hoops: Referees for tonight’s Spurs-OKC Game 7: Marc Davis, John Goble, Josh Tiven, Mitchell Ervin (alternate).

item.title: Devin Vassell said he's "more excited than anything" …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Devin Vassell said he's "more excited than anything" with Game 7 of the Western Conference finals just hours away: "We have a chance to do something special...We're just going to come out and be aggressive and be physical and be the first one to punch."

item.title: I was trying to get as much sleep as possible.”
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Devin Vassell on how he slept last night: “I was tossing turning for a little bit, but I know I got to get good sleep because I know they’re going to be playing music out there at that (Scissortail) park trying to keep us up. I was trying to get as much sleep as possible.”

item.title: Vassell on Sweeney getting Orlando job: "Definitely …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Vassell on Sweeney getting Orlando job: "Definitely happy for him. The impact he's had here has been tremendous, challenging everybody to be better on defensive end, holding us to a certain standard. I wish him all the luck, but right now we're focused on him being here now."

item.title: "I (knew) I got to get good sleep because I (knew) …
item.description: "I was tossing and turning a little bit for sure," Vassell said Saturday morning at shootaround at the Paycom Center. Adding to the pressure: Vassell expected a lot of noise to be coming from Scissortail Park close to the team hotel. "I (knew) I got to get good sleep because I (knew) they were going to be playing music out there at that park trying to keep us up. I was trying to get as much sleep as possible," he said.

item.title: Jalen Williams finished Game 6 without any setbacks
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Mark Daigneault says Jalen Williams didn’t suffer any setback in Game 6, but acknowledges that he didn’t undergo the normal return-to-play procedure leading into his return. Says “all the stakeholders” huddled before the game before he gave it a go, and did the same afterward. Says Williams actually felt good after Game 6 relative to his recovery. Notes that if OKC should advance, he’ll continue his rehab and they’ll move forward with the same process.

item.title: “Stomach's a little empty, and head feels clear. …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Asked Mitch Johnson how he’s feeling for Game 7 “Stomach's a little empty, and head feels clear. Hopefully we can drink enough water and breathe slowly and enjoy it, and have some fun, and go get out there and compete our butts off.” He also spoke on G7 experience and advice:

item.title: Played this team a lot this year and recently, so a …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Mitch on how he and his staff have approached things since end of Game 6: “I think we've handled it appropriately. Played this team a lot this year and recently, so a lot of attention to detail, but not a whole lot of new for the most part, at least on the surface level.”

item.title: “He’s had many opportunities of potentially becoming a …
item.description: “Thrilled for Sean. He deserves it,” Johnson said Saturday before the Spurs played the Oklahoma City Thunder in Game 7 of the Western Conference finals. “He’s had many opportunities of potentially becoming a head coach and so it was a matter of when, not if, even when I reached out to him to speak with him for the first time.”

item.title: I mean, he’s challenged everybody to be better on the …
item.description: “Definitely happy for him,” Spurs guard Devin Vassell said. “The impact that he’s had here has been tremendous. I mean, he’s challenged everybody to be better on the defensive end. He’s holding us to a certain standard. You know, I wish him all the luck.”

item.title: Teams with a point differential above 11 had never …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Teams with a point differential above 11 had never lost a series against teams with a point differential under 11. Until tonight. At the 24th try, San Antonio becomes the first one to pull it off.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama wins Western Conference MVP
item.description: NBA Communications: San Antonio Spurs forward-center Victor Wembanyama is the recipient of the Earvin “Magic” Johnson Trophy as the Most Valuable Player of the 2026 Western Conference Finals.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama received all nine votes from a media …
item.description: NBA Communications: Victor Wembanyama received all nine votes from a media panel covering the Western Conference Finals. The voting panel

item.title: VICTOR WEMBANYAMA
item.description: HoopsHype: VICTOR WEMBANYAMA Youngest Conference Finals MVP. Youngest Defensive Player of the Year. Youngest player with both All-NBA 1st Team and All-Defensive 1st Team selections. You could say he's ahead of schedule.

item.title: Six of the seven Spurs who played 10+ minutes tonight …
item.description: Will Guillory: Six of the seven Spurs who played 10+ minutes tonight are 26 or younger. These dudes are just getting started.

item.title: Let's say it again... "The Spurs are the Western …
item.description: Manu Ginobili: Let's say it again... "The Spurs are the Western Conference Champions!". My first one as a fan!

item.title: Spurs win Game 7, advance to NBA Finals
item.description: Clemente Almanza: FINAL: Spurs 111, Thunder 103 SGA - 35 points, 9 assists Wallace - 17 points, 7 rebounds Williams - 11 points, 10 rebounds Wembanyama - 22 points, 7 rebounds Castle - 16 points, 6 assists Champagnie - 20 points, 6 rebounds San Antonio wins 4-3

item.title: This was a lottery team a year ago, then reloaded with …
item.description: Jared Weiss: A comeback for the ages. The Spurs are heading to the NBA Finals in one of the great seasons in recent memory. This was a lottery team a year ago, then reloaded with a healthy Victor Wembanyama and have become a juggernaut.

item.title: Only player in NBA history has had more three-pointers …
item.description: Jordan Howenstine: Only player in NBA history has had more three-pointers in a Conference Finals Game 7 than Julian Champagnie's 6 he has right now: Stephen Curry (7)

item.title: 43 straight wins now for teams in a Game 7 when …
item.description: Justin Russo: 43 straight wins now for teams in a Game 7 when leading by at least 3 points going to the fourth quarter.

item.title: NBA will have a different NBA champion for 8th …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: The NBA will have a different NBA champion for 8th straight season, extending the longest streak in league history. 2019 - Toronto 2020 - Los Angeles Lakers 2021 - Milwaukee 2022 - Golden State 2023 - Denver 2024 - Boston 2025 - Oklahoma City 2026 - New York/San Antonio

item.title: "They’re young, talented, well-coached, play the right …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: SGA on San Antonio as a potential longtime road block: "They’re young, talented, well-coached, play the right way, seems like they like each other. They have the make-up. You don’t beat us without the make-up."

item.title: "I don't know who has as much experience as we do this …
item.description: Tom Petrini: "I don't know who has as much experience as we do this year, 2025-26" -Mitch Johnson

item.title: "WE WANT FOUR MORE. WE ARE NOT DONE."
item.description: SportsCenter: "WE WANT FOUR MORE. WE ARE NOT DONE." Wemby knows the job is not finished

item.title: Spurs won 56 total games in Wembanyama's first two NBA …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Spurs won 56 total games in Wembanyama's first two NBA seasons. In his third season: 74 wins & an NBA Finals appearance.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama on playing the NBA Finals: 'It's a lifetime chance'
item.description: Michael Scotto: Victor Wembanyama: “Winning the Larry O'Brien is a childhood dream and having a real shot at it, having a chance, a tangible chance at winning it, realizing a dream, it's a lifetime chance. You never know what's going to happen again. The day we win it, it’s going to be an amazing day of realization of a dream. It's almost like the meaning of my life.”

item.title: "(Pop) goes through some things we can't even imagine. …
item.description: Oh No He Didn’t: Wemby: "(Pop) goes through some things we can't even imagine. I need to call him. I need to see him, I need to talk to him because there's no way I can understand right now how he feels. When I talk to him, it's gonna be only a story in my head, except if I record it in secret but I need to talk him so quick"

item.title: Wemby on Julian Champagnie: "Julian's amazing. He …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Wemby on Julian Champagnie: "Julian's amazing. He deserves everything that he gets. He got cut in the NBA few years back. He's had tough moments...now he's taking this team to the NBA Finals"

item.title: De'Aaron Fox, in the Finals: "Coming here, my first …
item.description: Tom Petrini: De'Aaron Fox, in the Finals: "Coming here, my first full year, being able to be in the Finals, I don't know about if I thought that, but I knew that we're going to have a damn good team, and we continued to get better throughout the course of the season. While we were winning games, we were getting better every step of the way, and by the time we got to, I would even say, like, All-Star break, I was like, 'it could be real, we definitely have a chance this year.'"

item.title: Luke Kornet, on the reaction to his game-changing …
item.description: Spurs Nation: Spurs center Luke Kornet, on the reaction to his game-changing chase-down block: “Someone from the bench yelled, ‘Who is it? LeBron James?’ We’ll see which one has more staying power in the record books of history.”

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: 'The Spurs were just the better team tonight, from start to finish'
item.description: NBA Base: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander after the Thunder’s Game 7 loss to the Spurs "They were just the better team tonight, from start to finish. And every time we tried to, like, cut into it and take control of the game, it felt like they had an answer. And a lot of times it felt like it was like tough shot-making, so hats off to them. Yeah, they just, they played better tonight and they deserved to win the game."

item.title: “At the end of the day, it’s about us trying to win a …
item.description: Ahmed/The Ears/IG: BigBizTheGod: Chet Holmgren when asked about battling Wemby: “At the end of the day, it’s about us trying to win a basketball game. The Thunder trying to win a basketball game.”

item.title: Alex Caruso, asked if the Spurs feel like a problem …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Alex Caruso, asked if the Spurs feel like a problem that needs to be solved: “There’s nothing that needs to be solved. We could’ve won the game tonight. You would’ve been asking them the same thing. I don’t think there’s this narrative that this is a bugaboo. We should’ve played better and won the game and been in the NBA Finals. They’re a good team, they’re young. (We’ll) both probably be around for a while. But we’ve gotta get better and try and win next time.”

item.title: “They’re a good team. Beatable team. Obviously, we …
item.description: Brandon Rahbar: Cason Wallace on the Spurs: “They’re a good team. Beatable team. Obviously, we beat them. But we gotta do things better for a longer period of time.”

item.title: Back to back marathon takes a toll and it did on them …
item.description: Eddie A Johnson: I said before the season started that the @okcthunder would be tested and not because of lack of skill, heart and desire but because the back to back marathon takes a toll and it did on them physically against the young @spurs. Parity is here and it’s a beautiful thing!

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo sweepstakes reopening for Thunder after Game 7 loss to Spurs?
item.description: Still, Gilgeous-Alexander referenced the reality that Presti isn’t one to stand still. He analyzes. He innovates. He corrects. If he learned anything from the legendary Gregg Popovich and famed Spurs executive R.C. Buford during his early years with the Spurs that led to his Thunder rise, it was that. League sources have long maintained that Oklahoma City would not take part in the Giannis Antetokounmpo sweepstakes that will dominate the summertime conversation, but this sort of stunning finish

item.title: OKC’s summer comes with decisions. Holmgren and Jalen …
item.description: “The whole league now is trying to beat (OKC). That means they’re building their teams that way,” Kerr said. “San Antonio, they got young talent, they got Wemby, and they have really good and strong defensive guards. It was like, ‘Oh, s—.’ All of a sudden, they got a rival. I don’t know who the next young team who’s going to be doing that is, but you better believe when all these teams are building their rosters, they got the Thunder in mind, because they know for the next six, eight years, the

item.title: In this Western Conference finals series, the matchup …
item.description: In four regular-season games against the Spurs, Chet Holmgren made only 38.7 percent of his shots. He scored just 42 combined points. In the Thunder’s December loss to the Spurs during the NBA Cup, he fared slightly better, with 17 points and seven rebounds — but it still wasn’t as good as Wembanyama’s 22 points and nine rebounds (in only 21 minutes). In this Western Conference finals series, the matchup wasn’t close. Eight points, 13 points, 14 points, 10 points, 16 points, 10 points and, in Ga

item.title: “It’s the first time you’re facing the NBA playoffs …
item.description: But there was a moment in the early second quarter of Game 7 when he was leaning, quite literally, on Bismack Biyombo for advice. His vet gave him a speech about how he was playing and carrying himself. The what, which he knows, but more importantly, the why and the how, which he’s learning. “It’s the first time you’re facing the NBA playoffs with somebody else on the other side that won the MVP that you were supposed to win — that’s even more pressure. It brings joy,” Biyombo told The Athletic.

item.title: Jeremy Sochan set to receive 2026 NBA ring regardless of Finals outcome
item.description: New York Knicks forward Jeremy Sochan is already set to receive a 2026 NBA championship ring before the Finals even begin. Regardless of whether the Knicks or the San Antonio Spurs win the series, Sochan is expected to receive the reward. Typically, NBA championship rings are awarded not only to players on the Finals-winning team, but also to any players who appeared for that franchise during the regular season. Sochan started the season with the Spurs, appearing in 28 games before being waived.

item.title: Every time someone downplays Tim Duncan's impact, …
item.description: Paul Don't Lie: Every time someone downplays Tim Duncan's impact, they're either too young to have watched him (and are therefore basing it all on the stats), or they're stupid.

item.title: Spurs expected to decline Julian Champagnie's team option, re-sign him to better deal
item.description: Brian Windhorst on Julian Champagnie: Pretty good claim by the Spurs. They also signed him to an excellent contract which has another year on it as a team option. And I think they're going to decline his option and then re-sign him to a better contract.

item.title: Holmgren only took three total 3s the last four games …
item.description: Anthony Slater: Chet Holmgren only took three total 3s the last four games of the Spurs series. His 3-point volume has been decreasing. Mark Daigneault said it's a "priority" to correct that. But "Every minute Chet Holmgren has been on the team we've been the 1-seed in the Western Conference."

item.title: Most points by a player age 20 or younger in a single …
item.description: Polymarket Hoops: Most points by a player age 20 or younger in a single playoff run: 351 — Jayson Tatum (2018) 335 — Tyler Herro (2020) 293 — Magic Johnson (1980) 235 — Dylan Harper (2026)

item.title: Simmons on the Pelicans trading Trey Murphy: “We …
item.description: Garland Gillen: Bill Simmons on the Pelicans trading Trey Murphy: “We assume he’s probably going to get traded over the next three weeks. I think Boston is a possibility. We thought whoever lost this matchup (Spurs and Thunder) is a possibility. You have two to three weeks to trade for someone like that.”

item.title: Fans hoping to attend Game 1 of the NBA Finals between …
item.description: Fans hoping to attend Game 1 of the NBA Finals between the San Antonio Spurs and New York Knicks will need deep pockets, with resale ticket prices topping $1,200.

item.title: The cheapest options were located in the arena's …
item.description: According to a Ticketmaster listing, the lowest available resale tickets were listed at approximately $1,245 per seat as of Sunday. The cheapest options were located in the arena's upper-level seating sections, while premium seats were listed for more than $4,300 each before taxes.

item.title: We’re learning more details after a 17-year-old was …
item.description: We’re learning more details after a 17-year-old was hospitalized in critical condition after falling from a vehicle while celebrating a Spurs win. The teen, identified by a family member as Joey Rodriguez, remains in the hospital on life support as his doctors continue to run tests.

item.title: Yvonne Hudson, a family member of Joey's, says he was …
item.description: Yvonne Hudson, a family member of Joey's, says he was out celebrating with a few close friends when he fell from the vehicle after it hit a curb. His friends picked up him and rushed him to the nearest hospital before EMS arrived. He was later transferred to University Hospital with severe brain swelling.

item.title: Alex Caruso on when he felt San Antonio could be a …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Alex Caruso on when he felt San Antonio could be a real threat to OKC: "I didn't speculate just because so much happens in the course of the season, you know? One injury changes the team's entire season. (It's) the reason the Spurs got Dylan Harper. Wemby missed a whole bunch of games with the leg stuff. Sometimes you need the course of NBA seasons to shape up and see what the future is going to be. You can't always predict that."

item.title: Becky Hammon on the Spurs making the NBA Finals: …
item.description: Kendra Andrews: Becky Hammon on the Spurs making the NBA Finals: “Sweet, it's awesome…It takes a lot of people doing a lot of things right for to build that. That doesn't just happen overnight…. Obviously, you don't just have a franchise guy in Wemby, you have a generational guy. It’s been really fun to watch them play”

item.title: Josh Hart on how he reacts to centers — and, more …
item.description: Fred Katz: Josh Hart on how he reacts to centers — and, more specifically, Victor Wembanyama — guarding him so they can roam into the lane: "The only unanimous Defensive Player of the Year is guarding me, so that's a good sign for me, right? ... I'm a pretty good player."

item.title: Deuce McBride on the Spurs' young guards: "They're …
item.description: Fred Katz: Deuce McBride on the Spurs' young guards: "They're relentless. They're young. ... (They) just go out there and make it a tough game. I love that."

item.title: Jalen Williams on facing San Antonio: 'It honestly feels like we are playing a version of ourselves'
item.description: Anthony Slater: Jalen Williams on the Spurs: “It honestly feels like we are playing a version of ourselves.” Could he have changed the outcome? “I think we could’ve won if I played…But it’s also hats off to them. What do you want them to do about me being hurt?”

item.title: Chet Holmgren on how he plans to approach the …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Chet Holmgren on how he plans to approach the offseason after not playing to his standard against San Antonio: "I feel like it comes down to looking at the tape, figuring out where I can improve individually and then trusting my work through the offseason."

item.title: Chet Holmgren reflects on his struggles in the Spurs …
item.description: Anthony Slater: Chet Holmgren reflects on his struggles in the Spurs series: “I feel like there were definitely opportunities to get more attempts up that I didn't in the moment. That's an area to improve.”

item.title: Chet Holmgren on how much he'll tailor his offseason …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Chet Holmgren on how much he'll tailor his offseason toward preparing for the Spurs: "I definitely think it's different in terms of I don't think there's another team that has their play style and their personnel. So they're unique in that way. You can't just kind of play like a base normal, you know, this is what we do on an average Tuesday night type of thing. The way you have to attack it is look at the film, look at where you can improve and then work on the things that you

item.title: Josh Hart, one of the smartest players in the league, …
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Josh Hart, one of the smartest players in the league, was asked today at Knicks practice about what the team can learn from playing the Spurs in the NBA Cup final. "Technically, that game didn't happen," Hart said. (It didn't count in season standings or stats, so he's right.)

item.title: NBA Finals court designs will feature the Larry O'Brien Trophy
item.description: Matt Guzman: Here's a look at the courts both teams will use for the NBA Finals, affixed with the Larry O'Brien Trophy at the center. This looks awesome. #PorVida

item.title: For one bettor on DraftKings Sportsbook, however, a …
item.description: The Knicks finally know who their opponent will be following Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs’ monumental Game 7 closeout win over the Thunder on Saturday night. For one bettor on DraftKings Sportsbook, however, a $49,000 straight bet on the Knicks to win the NBA Finals for a massive $980,000 payout still hangs in the balance. The finals winner bet was made a day after the Knicks beat the Hawks in Round 1 on May 1, as they were priced with 19-to-1 odds to win the championship.

item.title: “I mean, that was December. I don't think that's …
item.description: Josh Hart, in fact, took the comedic route in explaining why there's nothing for the Knicks to glean from that game. “I mean, that was December. I don't think that's really gonna be equivalent to what the atmosphere and energy is going to be like,” Hart said, via SNY. “Technically, that game didn't happen, so I don't think there's anything that we can learn from.”

item.title: "I thought it was over, I ain't going to lie to you," …
item.description: Julian Champagnie's improbable journey from being unceremoniously cut by Philadelphia on Valentine's Day 2023 to reaching the NBA Finals as a key member of the Spurs came to mind as he held onto the ball at the end of Game 7 of the Western Conference Finals. "I thought it was over, I ain't going to lie to you," the forward said, recalling how emotional he was when the Sixers let him go as a 21-year-old undrafted rookie out of St. John's.

item.title: "My teammates do a really good job of keeping me in …
item.description: Stephen Curry is the only player in NBA history with more 3-pointers in a conference finals Game 7. "My teammates do a really good job of keeping me in check and making sure I don't get too high and never get too low, so I give all that to them honestly and truly," he said. "That's something I've been working on for a long time now, trying to stay even keeled. I've had my ups and downs with it, but I feel like this year my teammates really did pull me together and tell me, 'We need you, so never

item.title: “They definitely want to beat me and I want to kick …
item.description: Now, Brown, 56, is heading back to a city where his family still resides, needing to defeat the friends and the franchise that helped catapult his career to claim his first championship as a head coach. “They definitely want to beat me and I want to kick their ass,” Brown said following Sunday’s practice. “You love ’em and you can always love ’em before and after … I got ties to San Antonio and you appreciate the people, you appreciate the journey and all that other stuff. But at the end of the

item.title: He got rid of a lot of the people that had connections …
item.description: Brian Berger on Blazers owner Tom Dundon: "He's been a bull in a china shop so far. He's come in and just cut costs. He didn't allow t-shirts. I look at teams like the Knicks and the Spurs and you see all the former players sitting courtside and how the teams have embraced the former players. He got rid of a lot of the people that had connections to the former players last week when they laid off 70 people. He's cutting hotel costs. He's running this kind of like he runs the Carolina Hurricanes,

item.title: Sean Sweeney has officially been named Orlando’s next …
item.description: ‪Jason Beede‬: New: Sean Sweeney has officially been named Orlando’s next head coach, the #Magic announced. Sweeney will stick with the Spurs through the Finals. “Sean brings a tremendous work ethic and a high degree of intensity that set the tone for everything he does,” Jeff Weltman said.

item.title: He agreed to a four-year deal with the Magic that …
item.description: Sweeney, 41, will continue with the Spurs through the NBA Finals, the Magic said. San Antonio has homecourt advantage when it takes on the New York Knicks in the Finals, which start Wednesday night on ABC. He agreed to a four-year deal with the Magic that includes an additional one-year team option, a league source confirmed to the Orlando Sentinel. Sports Illustrated first reported the length of Sweeney’s contract.

item.title: NBAers in for Canada this summer:
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: To sum up NBAers in for Canada this summer: SGA, NAW, Barrett, Brooks, Dort, Nembhard bros, Mathurin, Olynyk, George, Miller Out this summer but in for this cycle: Edey, Powell Didn’t commit: Murray, Sharpe, Wiggins Committed but not selected: Lawson, Banton, Houstan, Prosper

item.title: Jeremy Sochan on how to get Victor Wembanyama tired: Give him different looks, be very physical and make him run
item.description: Jeremy Sochan signed with the Knicks after clearing waivers, and now his new team will face off against his old team in the NBA Finals. “Whether I’m playing or not, it’s important to feed all the information I have. And I think I know quite a lot,” Sochan said in an exclusive interview with Jared Schwartz of the New York Post. “Being that tall [Wembanyama] gets tired,” Sochan continued. “Give him different looks and [not beat him up but] be very physical, and make him run. And he’s gonna get tir

item.title: He said he sold around 2,000 shirts during the 2014 …
item.description: Others skipped the wait by shopping with vendors set up along Southwest Military. Garry Ortega, who has been selling merchandise for about 40 years, said demand is already outpacing the Spurs’ last Finals run. He said he sold around 2,000 shirts during the 2014 Spurs Final season, and that this year he has already sold about 4,000. Ortega said, “Wemby is the number one seller of course.”

item.title: In the hours after the win, some fans headed to …
item.description: In the hours after the win, some fans headed to Academy Sports for Spurs Finals gear. The store opened its doors after the victory to long lines of customers eager to buy Finals T-shirts and hats. Academy had boxes of pre-printed items ready to go, and one fan who waited about half an hour to reach the front of the line said, “Im glad we got here when we did cause there's already a line to get in so we just beat the line to get in the store its ok we love our Spurs.”
